title: Maximizing Heap Operations with Min and Max Priority Queues

statement.markdown:
Given two priority queues (max PQ for 'max' and min PQ for 'min') implemented as heaps, perform the following operations on the arrays of integers provided. The goal is to maximize the number of heap operations (insert, deleteMax, deleteMin).

Your goal is to write a function that maximizes the number of heap operations, and to do so in the given time constraint, with the given space complexity constraint.

Example: [3, 6, 1, 10] - The optimal solution would be to insert 3, 6 and 10 into the max PQ (maxPQ = [6, 10, 3]) and 1 into the min PQ.

You can do this with the following sequence of operations: Insert 3 into max PQ, Insert 6 into max PQ, Insert 10 into max PQ, Insert 1 into min PQ, DeleteMax from max PQ, DeleteMin from min PQ, DeleteMax from max PQ.

Note: The goal of the problem is to find the optimal sequence of operations.
statement.inputFormat: Three space-separated integers representing the input arrays for [max PQ, min PQ, and the array to process].
statement.outputFormat: Three space-separated integers representing the final values in max PQ, min PQ, and the array to process, respectively.
statement.notes: Your function should return the final state of max PQ, min PQ, and the array to process. The goal is to find the optimal sequence of operations.

constraints.description: Size of max PQ input array
Size of min PQ input array
Size of array to process input array

examples.explanation: Insert 10 into max PQ, Insert 5 into min PQ, Insert 6 into max PQ, DeleteMax from max PQ, DeleteMin from min PQ, DeleteMax from max PQ.

hints.text: Try to find a solution using a single data structure instead of two.

editorial.title: Solution Title
editorial.intuition:
The optimal solution is to use a single data structure, such as a min heap. We can insert all numbers from the input array into the min heap.

The idea is to maintain a counter for max PQ elements. When we pop a number from the min heap, if the counter is zero, we move the popped number to max PQ. If the counter is non-zero, we increment the counter.

This way, we ensure that the max PQ is always at least as big as the max PQ input array.
editorial.timeComplexity: O((n + m + p) log (n + m + p))
